_comment: Canonical registry of the CLI tools orc relies on. Consumed by both the SessionStart tool-check hook (orc/hooks/scripts/session-start-tool-check.sh, via jq) and the orc installer CLI (cli/internal/deps, which embeds a CI-verified mirror at cli/internal/deps/tools.json). Keep the two byte-identical — the validate.yml `static` job enforces it. Hint keys are platform ids (macos/debian/fedora/arch/linux) with a `default` fallback, matching platform detection in both consumers. `install` maps a package manager (brew/apt/dnf/pacman/npm) to the args passed after its install verb; omit a manager when there is no safe unattended recipe (the CLI then falls back to printing the hint).

hints.macos: brew install gh && gh auth login
hints.fedora: sudo dnf install -y gh && gh auth login
hints.arch: sudo pacman -S github-cli && gh auth login
hints.default: see https://cli.github.com/  — then: gh auth login

tools.name: agent-browser
tools.usedBy: `/orc:qa` (web mode — required for browser-driven QA evidence)

hints.macos: brew install --cask atlassian-acli  — then: acli jira auth login --web
hints.debian: see https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/acli/guides/how-to-get-started/  — then: acli jira auth login --web
hints.fedora: see https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/acli/guides/how-to-get-started/  — then: acli jira auth login --web
hints.arch: see https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/acli/guides/how-to-get-started/  — then: acli jira auth login --web
hints.linux: see https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/acli/guides/how-to-get-started/  — then: acli jira auth login --web

tools.name: docker
tools.usedBy: `/orc:env`, `/orc:qa`/`/orc:flow` env provisioning (host-mode fallback applies without it)

hints.macos: brew install --cask docker  (or OrbStack: brew install --cask orbstack)
